steps.title: Understand how adding a constant affects statistics
steps.content:
When every value in a dataset increases by the same amount, the mean shifts by that amount, but the range stays the same.

Why? Since range = max − min, if both endpoints increase by 14:
$$\text{new range} = (\text{max} + 14) - (\text{min} + 14) = \text{max} - \text{min} = \text{old range}$$

steps.title: Determine Set A's statistics from the dot plot
steps.content:
The dot plot shows capacities at 4, 7, 10, 13, and 16 µF.

For Set A:
  • Minimum: 4 µF
  • Maximum: 16 µF
  • Range = 16 − 4 = 12 µF
  • Mean = 10 µF (from counting dots and calculating)

steps.title: Apply the transformation to get Set B's statistics
steps.content:
Since each capacitor in Set B is 14 µF greater:
$$\text{Mean of Set B} = 10 + 14 = 24 \text{ µF}$$
$$\text{Range of Set B} = 12 \text{ µF}$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
